Against poster ground truth (read demoplan.png): panel 5 caption 'Vertebrae are addressable seats. Nerves carry the state' and panels 5-6 show tabs distributed on/around the BODY (would ride the orbit), focus pulled forward. The code's corner HUD is screen-decoupled instead. Caveat (why needs-decision, not objective-safe/bug): the inline comments label this the 'LOCKED ORCHESTRATION MODEL' and the auto-memory trail (obs 2643/2647/2667) shows the four-corner camera-anchored HUD was a deliberate, recently-authored choice replacing an earlier spine-descending layout — chosen partly so code stays legible from any orbit angle. So it is a designed departure, not an accidental wrong-mapping. Resolving it (re-seat on vertebrae vs. formally accept+document the HUD) is a composition/operator call, exactly as the proposedFix and fixClass state. Severity medium is right: posture-fidelity gap, no functional/accessibility impact.

area: motion — focus tab feeding during work
title: In 2+ orchestration the focus tab is fed by the spine plunging in (no umbilical) — matches poster's 'data flows down into the work'
detail: VERIFIED CORRECT (positive). For the HUD focus tab the umbilical is hidden and curveRef nulled so 'the FOCUS owns dead-center and the SPINE plunges straight into it' (MaterializedTab.tsx:1379-1386), and only WAITING tabs are nerve-tethered to their nearest vertebra (1387-1413). This matches poster panel 6's read of the brain/spine driving the focused work directly (state flows DOWN into the slab) rather than a redundant curved pipe arcing back out. The umbilical beads also accelerate with surfaceExcitation while working (1492-1496). Recorded so verify does not re-flag the missing-focus-umbilical as a defect.
proposedFix: No change. Density/brightness of the descending feed is the operator's RTX call (aesthetic-rtx).

title: Line-by-line reveal uses a fixed 90ms setInterval decoupled from metabolic 'working' rate; the 'showing work' pace doesn't track the body's flow
detail: The 'showing work' reveal (poster: the being writing/showing its work live) advances one content line every fixed 90ms via window.setInterval (MaterializedTab.tsx:1642-1646), independent of metabolism.phase / surfaceExcitation / posture.flow. Meanwhile the slab glow and umbilical beads DO speed up with surfaceExcitation (phaseRate 'working'=4.4 at 1169; beadFlowSpeed at 1495). So the visible writing cadence is constant while the rest of the body's flow is metabolic — a minor inconsistency in the 'state flows through the body' read. Purely a cadence-coupling nuance, not a wrong mapping.
proposedFix: Optionally scale the reveal interval by posture.flow / metabolism rate so the writing speed tracks the working posture (faster when streaming). Aesthetic/feel pacing — operator's RTX call on exact timing.

title: Motes return to a hardcoded point, not the (crowning, shrinking) brain — poster shows energy flowing UP into the brain
detail: ReabsorptionParticles is mounted with target={[0, 0.1, 0]} (MaterializationLayer.tsx line 437), a fixed brain-group-local point. The poster panel 7 caption 'Energy returns up the spine' shows motes streaming from the work vertebra all the way UP into the brain crown. But the actual brain is rendered inside brainVisualRef which scales DOWN to mainBrainScale 0.34–0.82 while orchestrating (livingWorkspaceLayout.ts:214-223) and the brain GLB occupies brain-visual-local y in [-0.222, 0.633] (centroid ~0.2), all inside a group offset to position (0, 0.12, -1.2) (SuperbrainScene.tsx:832). The spine cord-top welds at the brainstem centroid (~the bottom 4% of the brain, BrainPointField.tsx:102). So [0,0.1,0] lands roughly at the brainstem/upper-spine join — well BELOW the visible brain centroid, and it does not track the brain as it shrinks/crowns. The motes therefore terminate at mid-body, never visibly entering the brain mass the poster depicts. This is a wrong coordinate mapping, not a feel choice.
proposedFix: Compute the target from the actual brain centroid: derive it from the fused brain bounds (or reuse the brainstem-anchor centroid already computed in BrainPointField/extremeCentroid and multiply by the published getBrainDockScale()), so the motes terminate inside the visible brain crown and follow it as it shrinks/rises. At minimum lift the target Y to the scaled brain centroid (~0.2 * dockScale) rather than the fixed 0.1.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED by reading the code. ReabsorptionParticles is mounted with a hardcoded target={[0,0.1,0]} (MaterializationLayer.tsx:434-440). MaterializationLayer renders as a child of the OUTER groupRef (SuperbrainScene.tsx:947, group at :886) and a SIBLING of brainVisualRef (:887) — the group that scales to mainBrainScale (:876-878). So the target lives in outer-group/brain-visual-local space and does NOT inherit the brain's shrink. The fused spine welds at the brainstem centroid = lowest 4% of the brain (extremeCentroid(...,lowest=true,0.04), BrainPointField.tsx:102), with the brain mass extending UP from there. A fixed y=0.1 therefore lands at roughly the brainstem/lower brain — below the visible brain centroid at rest — so motes stop short of the brain crown that poster panel 7 ('energy returns up the spine') depicts entering the brain. This is an objective wrong-coordinate mapping (a magic constant that doesn't track the visible brain), not a feel/RTX choice, so objective-safe holds. TWO corrections to the finding's reasoning: (1) The proposed fix ('multiply by getBrainDockScale() because the brain rises') is half-wrong for the POINTS being (the official frontend). In points mode the outer group is pinned static at (0,0.12,-1.2) (SuperbrainScene.tsx:831-832); mainBrainOffsetY/dockYRef is applied ONLY in the mesh else-branch (:853-857). The crown effect comes solely from brainVisualRef shrinking toward the origin, so the visible centroid DESCENDS toward ~0.2*mainBrainScale as it shrinks — moving CLOSER to (and past) the fixed target during orchestration. (2) Consequently the mismatch is WORST at rest/1-tab (centroid ~0.2 vs target 0.1) and inverts at high tab counts — the opposite of the finding's 'never visibly enters, terminates at mid-body always.' Correct fix: set target.y to the scaled visible brain centroid (~centroidY * mainBrainScale, tracking the shrink), NOT a rise that doesn't occur in points mode. Severity lowered high->medium: the offset is modest (~0.1 below centroid at rest), the motes still travel the correct direction up the spine and converge to the centerline, the artifact is a 1.3s transient, and it partly self-corrects while orchestrating. Fix must also be mirrored to the lab copy GAG demo\gag-orchestrator\src\components\canvas\MaterializationLayer.tsx (confirmed present).

title: Mote color #5ef0b0 deviates from the sacred tetrad green #54f0a0 and bypasses the 'complete' posture binding
detail: ReabsorptionParticles defaults color='#5ef0b0' (ReabsorptionParticles.tsx:33) and MaterializationLayer mounts it WITHOUT passing a color (lines 434-440), so the hardcoded #5ef0b0 is what renders. The sacred poster tetrad green is #54f0a0 (superbrain.css:31 --neon-green; bodyPosture BODY_POSTURES.complete = [84,240,160] = #54f0a0). The lifecycle already maps reabsorbing -> complete -> #54f0a0 (bodyPosture.ts:60), and MaterializationLayer already derives bodyPosture for this exact phase (line 396) but never feeds it to the motes. #5ef0b0 (94,240,176) is a distinct, brighter/cyan-shifted green that is OFF the sacred tetrad — a palette deviation to FLAG, plus a dead binding: the body's own 'complete' status color is computed and ignored, breaking the 'STATUS FROM BODY' legend.
proposedFix: Pass the live posture color into ReabsorptionParticles: color={`rgb(${posture.color.join(',')})`} (or postureColor01 -> THREE.Color) so the motes wear the body's #54f0a0 complete hue. Replace the #5ef0b0 default with #54f0a0. This is a mapping/palette correction, not an intensity tune.

verdict.reason: Confirmed by reading the cited files. ReabsorptionParticles.tsx defaults color='#5ef0b0' (the prop default, ~line 25/33), and MaterializationLayer.tsx:434-440 mounts <ReabsorptionParticles origin/target/startedAt/durationMs> with NO color prop, so the hardcoded #5ef0b0 (94,240,176) is what renders. That hue is OFF the sacred tetrad: superbrain.css:31 sets --neon-green #54f0a0, and bodyPosture.ts:40 defines complete = [84,240,160] = #54f0a0; #5ef0b0 is +10 red / +16 blue — a distinctly brighter, cyan-shifted green that matches none of the four poster hues. The dead-binding claim also holds: bodyPosture.ts:60 maps reabsorbing -> complete, and MaterializationLayer.tsx:396 derives bodyPosture in scope, yet it is never passed to the motes — so the body's own 'complete' status color is computed and ignored, breaking the 'STATUS FROM BODY' legend for poster phase 7. This is a hue/chroma deviation off the sacred tetrad, NOT a brightness/density/glow intensity dial, so it is not the operator's RTX call; the correct target hue is already canonically defined (#54f0a0), making the fix a deterministic mapping/palette correction requiring no aesthetic judgment. Classification objective-safe is correct. Severity downgraded high->medium: the violation is real but its footprint is a small, transient, additive-blended <=72-mote effect that fades over 1.3s in one phase, less impactful than persistent off-tetrad chrome.

title: Motes rise on a straight lerp and ignore the spine's forward bow — poster motes travel UP the spine, not through empty space
detail: The mote motion lerps each point straight from origin to target with only a lateral 'conv' convergence toward the X/Z centerline (ReabsorptionParticles.tsx:69-73); Y is a plain linear interpolation (line 72). The poster caption is explicitly 'Energy returns up the spine', and the real spine bows forward (z = CORD_Z + sin(f*PI)*0.04, CORD_Z=-0.42; spineAnatomy.ts:3,16-24) descending from y -0.92 to -2.85. Because the motes converge to the spine CENTERLINE in X but interpolate Z straight toward the target Z (~0), and rise on a straight line, their path is a chord through space rather than a current that hugs the curved cord. The 'up the spine' read the poster sells (motes visibly tracking the vertebral column) is weakened to a generic rise-and-fade.
proposedFix: Route the Y-rise along the spine: sample the fused cord curve (or interpolate Z with the same sin-bow as SEGMENT_ANCHORS) so motes follow z = fuse(CORD_Z + sin(f*PI)*0.04) as they ascend, keeping X near the cord centerline. Bias the rise to pass through 1-2 vertebra waypoints between origin and the brain target.
locator: ReabsorptionParticles.tsx:60-74 (linear lerp + lateral conv); spineAnatomy.ts:3,16-24 (cord bow); CORD_Z=-0.42

verdict.reason: Confirmed by reading the code. ReabsorptionParticles.tsx lines 60-73 move each mote with: Y as a plain linear lerp `sy + (target[1]-sy)*e` (line 72, no spine-curve sampling), X converging toward target[0] via `conv` (line 71), and Z lerping toward target[2] via the same `conv` (line 73). MaterializationLayer.tsx:437 passes `target={[0, 0.1, 0]}` (brain crown, Z=0); origin is the fused vertebra anchor (getContentSurfacePlacement → fuseSpinePoint of SEGMENT_ANCHORS[seat] + SEAT_FEED_OFFSET), whose Z sits at the visible cord depth (raw CORD_Z=-0.42 + sin(f*PI)*0.04, then *spineScale+weld). So Z does pull straight from cord depth toward 0 and Y rises on a straight line: the path is a chord from one vertebra to the brain, not a current tracing the bowed cord through intervening vertebrae. The component imports nothing from spineAnatomy/spineFusionBus and samples no cord curve, so this is NOT already handled — not a false positive or misread. The detail's mechanical description (linear Y, lateral conv, straight Z toward ~0) is accurate. Caveat justifying my reclass and a softer severity: the motion direction is already correct (motes rise, converge to the X≈0 centerline, and home into the brain), and the cord's forward bow is tiny (0.04 raw, further shrunk by spineScale<1), so the 'up the spine' read is weakened, not broken. The proposed fix (route the rise along the fused cord curve and bias through 1-2 vertebra waypoints) is a posture/motion-FEEL choice — how hard to hug the curve and how many waypoints — not a single-right-answer wrong-mapping; the target is already correctly the brain. That makes it a product/operator motion call rather than a no-judgment objective fix.

title: No distinct 'nerves retract' beat before dissolve — slab retract + motes fire concurrently, collapsing the poster's 4-step sequence
detail: Poster panel 7 is a 4-5 frame filmstrip with ordered beats: Work completes -> Nerves retract -> Tab dissolves into particles -> Energy returns up the spine -> Back to rest. In code, a single retractProgress over 1300ms simultaneously shrinks reachProgress, slabProgress AND liveProgress (MaterializedTab.tsx:1231-1242) while ReabsorptionParticles plays its own 1300ms rise concurrently. The umbilical/conductor nerves just fade with the same retractProgress — there is no separate, earlier 'nerves retract / detach' gesture preceding the slab dissolve. The phases the poster sequences in time are overlapped into one cross-fade, so the narrative reads as a generic fade-out rather than retract-then-dissolve-then-ascend.
proposedFix: Stage the retract: phase 1 (~0-30%) collapse the umbilical/conductor roots (tube drawRange/opacity -> 0) as the 'nerves retract' beat; phase 2 (~25-70%) dissolve the slab body into the mote spawn; phase 3 (~50-100%) motes ascend the spine. Offset ReabsorptionParticles startedAt / birth window so the ascent begins after the nerves have visibly detached.
locator: MaterializedTab.tsx:1231-1242 (single retractProgress drives reach/slab/live together); MaterializationLayer.tsx:430-441 (motes start at tab.phaseStartedAt, same t0)

verdict.reason: Confirmed by reading all three cited locations. MaterializedTab.tsx:1231-1242: in the 'retracting' lifecycle a SINGLE retractProgress (elapsed / 1300ms) drives reachProgress, slabProgress AND liveProgress as identical 1-retractProgress curves. The umbilical/conductor nerves derive their drawRange from reachProgress (lines 1248, 1278) and opacity from Math.max(reachProgress, slabProgress) (1265, 1291), so they fade on the exact same curve as the slab body — there is no earlier, isolated 'nerves retract / detach' gesture. MaterializationLayer.tsx:438-439: ReabsorptionParticles starts at tab.phaseStartedAt (same t0 as the retract) with durationMs=1300 (identical window), and ReabsorptionParticles.tsx:61-63 begins the mote ascent from t=0 (birth stagger only 0..0.45, no detach offset), so motes rise concurrently with the slab dissolve and nerve fade. The poster's phase-7 filmstrip is an EXPLICIT captioned, time-ordered storyboard: 'Work completes -> Nerves retract -> Tab dissolves into particles -> Energy returns up the spine -> Back to rest', with 'Nerves retract' a discrete beat BEFORE 'Tab dissolves into particles'. The code collapses these documented ordered beats into one simultaneous 1300ms cross-fade, so the reabsorption reads as a generic fade-out rather than retract-then-dissolve-then-ascend. The claim is accurate, grounded, and the line locators are precise. Not already handled, not a misread. fixClass needs-decision is correct: the staging gap is real against a documented sequence, but the exact phase split (0-30/25-70/50-100), easing, and whether to fully phase-gate vs. lightly offset are choreography/feel choices the operator should make (the timing intensity also overlaps the RTX fidelity dimension), so it is not a mechanical objective-safe fix.

title: Motes spawn from a fixed vertebra-feed point, not from the dissolving slab's body (orchestration places the focus slab as a camera-anchored HUD element, far from originLocal)
detail: origin is tab.originLocal (MaterializationLayer.tsx:436) — the SEAT_FEED_OFFSET vertebra-feed point (materializedSurfaceAnchors.ts:63), correct as the spine attach point. But the poster shows the SLAB ITSELF dissolving into particles. In orchestration (2+ tabs) the focused slab is rendered as a camera-anchored HUD element at screen-center/forward (isHudTab, HUD_DIST=6.0; MaterializedTab.tsx:46-49,987) and the points-focus single tab is a lateral cortex peer — both visually detached from originLocal. So the motes appear to emanate from an empty vertebra rather than from where the slab visibly is, breaking the 'tab dissolves into particles' read for the very tab being reabsorbed.
proposedFix: Spawn the motes from the slab's actual rendered pose (pose.targetLocal / HUD-projected position) for the first ~30% of the rise, then converge them onto the vertebra-feed origin before ascending the spine — so particles are seen leaving the slab, then flowing to the cord, then up to the brain.

title: Mote count (72) and size/opacity are an RTX-fidelity call, not an objective gap
detail: COUNT=72 motes, pointsMaterial size=0.05, opacity peaks ~0.95 with additive blending (ReabsorptionParticles.tsx:14,83-90). The poster's reabsorption stream reads as a denser, brighter ribbon of motes flowing up the column. Whether 72 is enough to sell that is a brightness/density judgment that belongs to the operator's RTX fidelity dial, not a correctness bug. Flagging only so it is not mistaken for objective; do NOT change count/size/opacity as a 'fix' without the operator's call.
proposedFix: Leave as-is unless the operator wants more drama; if so, raise COUNT (e.g. 120-180) and/or size for a denser ascending current. Classify any such change as aesthetic-rtx.
